description: Install the uv Python package manager
explanation: uv is a fast Python package manager. Solo CLI requires it for dependency management. Config lives under ~/.solo after install.

validation.rule: uv --version must return a version string

common_errors: curl not found on Windows — use PowerShell variant instead
PATH not updated — restart shell or run: source ~/.bashrc

description: Create a Python 3.12 virtual environment using uv
explanation: Creates an isolated Python 3.12 environment in .venv/. Recommended version for solo-cli compatibility.

validation.rule: .venv/ directory must exist in current working directory

common_errors: Python 3.12 not found — uv will attempt to fetch it automatically
Ran in wrong directory — always run from your project root

explanation: Activates the venv so all subsequent pip/uv commands install into it, not system Python.

validation.rule: VIRTUAL_ENV environment variable must be set

common_errors: Forgot to create venv first — run create_venv before this
.venv not in current dir — ensure you're in the project root

command.from_source: git clone https://github.com/GetSoloTech/solo-cli.git && cd solo-cli && uv pip install -e .

descriptions.from_source: Clone from GitHub and install in editable mode — recommended. Updates via git pull, no reinstall needed.
descriptions.from_pypi: Install a release snapshot from PyPI — quick but tracks releases, not latest commits

explanation: Installs the solo-cli tool. GitHub source install (editable mode) is recommended — it stays current with git pull. PyPI installs a fixed release snapshot.

validation.rule: solo --help must return command list including: setup, robo, serve, status, list, test, stop, download, setup-usb

common_errors: venv not activated — activate first or solo command won't be found
Upgrading from solo-server — uninstall solo-server first, migrate ~/.solo_server to ~/.solo (see MIGRATION.md)

description: Grant USB serial access for robot arms on Linux
explanation: Adds the current user to the dialout group for serial access to Koch/SO100/SO101 arms. Only needed on Linux. Informational-only on macOS.

validation.rule: User must be in dialout group (check: groups $USER)

common_errors: Must log out and back in after for group change to take effect
On macOS — this command is informational only, no action needed
